channel.title: Cariboo Signals
channel.copyright: © 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
channel.itunes:subtitle: Technology and society in rural BC with Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:author: Erich Zirnhelt
channel.itunes:summary:
How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Riley brings tech systems thinking with rural roots, while Casey focuses on community development. New episodes every day with weekly themes.
Community Accountability: Cariboo Signals scores 18/20 (Exemplary) on the Community Content Compact — Track A (Community-Insider production). Transparency: 4/4 · Source Integrity: 3/3 · Displacement: 2/2 · Consent & Attribution: 3/4 · Benefit Flow: 4/5 · Accountability: 2/2. Produced by Erich Zirnhelt, Horsefly, BC (Secwépemc territory). Corrections and PSA opt-outs: feedback@cariboosignals.ca.
channel.description: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Community Content Compact: 18/20 Exemplary (Track A). Self-funded, non-commercial. Producer: Erich Zirnhelt, Horsefly, BC. Corrections: feedback@cariboosignals.ca.

trace:assessment.trace:community: Rural Cariboo region, BC — Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh territories

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down Tod Maffin and [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Escaped Alone’ wins best production in Williams Lake, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural communities maintain authentic cultural voices and documentation when the infrastructure—connectivity, platforms, and AI tools—is controlled by external corporations? Topics include: Platform-independent publishing and the Tod Maffin/Canadaland models, Rural broadband infrastructure monopolies and the Rogers-Shaw merger enforcement gap, Community-owned broadband alternatives and the Olds, Alberta fiber co-op model, Indigenous cultural documentation and governance through tools like Mukurtu, Funding structures for cultural organizations and the project-versus-infrastructure mismatch.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Central Cariboo Arts & Culture Society

Sources discussed:

  • Newsletter: f***@mail.beehiiv.com: Tod Maffin - Official Web Site
  • Yashvika Grover (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Escaped Alone’ wins best production...
  • Ellsworth Toohey (Boing Boing): [Boing Boing] At a medieval church festival, the priest bray...
  • Ellsworth Toohey (Boing Boing): [Boing Boing] Kim Jong-il had a South Korean actress kidnapp...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] New bill introduces social media age...
  • Al Jazeera Staff (Al Jazeera English): [Al Jazeera English] US asks Anthropic to block global acces...
  • Jesse Brown (Canadaland): [Canadaland] Retraction and Apology to Theresa Kielburger

Additional sources provided:

  • Newsletter: f***@mail.beehiiv.com: What the Americans Said
  • Newsletter: f***@mail.beehiiv.com: Tod Maffin 🇨🇦 (@todmaffin.com)
  • Newsletter: c***@openmedia.org: Call Out the Broken Rogers-Shaw Deal
  • Newsletter: c***@openmedia.org: Call Out the Broken Rogers-Shaw Deal
  • Newsletter: c***@openmedia.org: Call Out the Broken Rogers-Shaw Deal
  • Daniel Cooper (Engadget): [Engadget] After Belfast riots, UK reminds social platforms ...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] We talked to the comedians behind the vir...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/malcolm-mcmillan (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] Critics hated parts of Denzel’s 1998 action thr...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Thousands mourn Thai princess as body t...
  • Jasper Mayone (Kagi Small Web): [Kagi Small Web] Podroll
  • Al Jazeera Staff (Al Jazeera English): [Al Jazeera English] Six people killed as helicopters collid...
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: #1378 A Cohere Founder on What A.I. Critics Get Wrong
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: #185 Ottawa’s Killer Robot Problem
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: Discover Our Cell Phone Plans | Fizz

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Web Search: Brave Search API
Weather Data: Open-Meteo
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down Tod Maffin and [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Escaped Alone’ wins best production in Williams Lake, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural communities maintain authentic cultural voices and documentation when the infrastructure—connectivity, platforms, and AI tools—is controlled by external corporations? Topics include: Platform-independent publishing and the Tod Maffin/Canadaland models, Rural broadband infrastructure monopolies and the Rogers-Shaw merger enforcement gap, Community-owned broadband alternatives and the Olds, Alberta fiber co-op model, Indigenous cultural documentation and governance through tools like Mukurtu, Funding structures for cultural organizations and the project-versus-infrastructure mismatch.

item.title: Science, Wonder And The Natural World
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Engadget] Parker Solar Probe makes another flyby of the sun, solar energy bags a win, and more science stories and [Business Insider] Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei warned Mythos posed a national security threat. Washington just responded., and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural communities like the Cariboo build sustainable, locally-valuable scientific and experiential infrastructure around their natural assets without depending on external funding cycles? Topics include: Dark sky preservation and International Dark-Sky Association designation process, Citizen science platforms (Globe at Night) and data governance, Space weather infrastructure gaps in rural communities, Ecotourism economics and sustainability models versus grant-cycle dependency, Indigenous knowledge systems and territorial relationships to the sky.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Salvation Army Williams Lake

Sources discussed:

  • Cheyenne MacDonald (Engadget): [Engadget] Parker Solar Probe makes another flyby of the sun...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei warned Mythos ...
  • Patrick Davies (100 Mile Free Press): [100 Mile Free Press] District of 100 Mile House debates use...

Additional sources provided:

  • Steve Dent (Engadget): [Engadget] Anthropic backtracks on policy that 'sabotaged' r...
  • Kagi Small Web: [Kagi Small Web] Living life like a video game
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Workers are spending over 6 hours a week ...
  • Jasper Mayone (Kagi Small Web): [Kagi Small Web] Verify
  • Android Authority: [Android Authority] Reader survey suggests modern smartphone...
  • Helen Branswell (STAT News): [STAT News] WHO director-general is profoundly concerned aft...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Dahiyeh crowds rally in favour of Irani...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Yemen’s ‘Spider-Man’ dies while scaling...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Family mourn Indian sailor killed in US...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Funerals held after deadly Pakistani st...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Albania protests escalate over Kushner-...
  • EarthSky: [EarthSky] Tonight | EarthSky

Closing Music: “Julius Marx - Vices” by Julius Marx — indie, pop, folk. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Web Search: Brave Search API
Weather Data: Open-Meteo
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

Washington just responded., and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural communities like the Cariboo build sustainable, locally-valuable scientific and experiential infrastructure around their natural assets without depending on external funding cycles? item.title: Cariboo Local Affairs
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Local Affairs.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake rejects jail closure idea, seeks government support on repeat crime and [Boing Boing] Man jailed after throwing ninja star at woman, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can a provincial Seniors Advocate's visit to rural Cariboo communities meaningfully improve policy outcomes, or will it remain performative without sustained follow-up and community preparation? Topics include: Role of the BC Seniors Advocate and measurable past policy outcomes, Capacity gaps in local media and their impact on accountability reporting, Organizational preparation required for rural communities to effectively engage with provincial officials, The broader civic infrastructure of rural communities (agricultural shows, cultural events, volunteer capacity), Structural design failures in how provincial government reaches rural populations.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: CMHA South Cariboo

Sources discussed:

  • rebeccadyok (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake rejects jail closure i...
  • Rob Beschizza (Boing Boing): [Boing Boing] Man jailed after throwing ninja star at woman
  • none (100 Mile Free Press): [100 Mile Free Press] FORSTER: Mountain Spruce Community Cen...
  • Patrick Davies (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Senior’s Advocate of B.C. coming to ...
  • rebeccadyok (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] 5th annual Cariboo Classic Jr Steer ...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake writer shortlisted for...

Additional sources provided:

  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Inomin Mines continues exploration n...
  • Laisa Conde (Quesnel Cariboo Observer): [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] HOMETOWN HERO: Lisa McCargar keep...
  • Laisa Conde (Quesnel Cariboo Observer): [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Quesnel River Rush celebrates ear...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Alarm as Ebola spreads into new areas o...
  • https://www.nytimes.com/by/jancee-dunn (NYT Well): [NYT Well] Summer Challenge: Put Your Phone Away for Better ...
  • Lachlan Labere (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Condolences pour in following death ...
  • none (100 Mile Free Press): [100 Mile Free Press] CARIBOO OUTDOORS: Why are lakes stocke...

Closing Music: “To Die For” by JARA — pop, indie, rocknroll. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Web Search: Brave Search API

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Al Jazeera English] Bolivian farmers clash with police as ex-president vows resistance and [Boing Boing] In 1932, John W. Campbell imagined machines outliving humanity, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can wildlife management frameworks keep pace with changing wild spaces, particularly regarding grizzly population recovery and human encroachment in the Chilcotin? Topics include: Yellowstone camping ban and grizzly population recovery as management case study, Jurisdictional gaps between provincial wildlife management and Tŝilhqot'in Aboriginal title in the Chilcotin, Remote monitoring technology capabilities and cost-benefit analysis, Sustainability challenges of grant-dependent monitoring infrastructure, Pre-trip stewardship communication protocols as governance model.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Family Enrichment Centre

Sources discussed:

  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Bolivian farmers clash with police as e...
  • Ellsworth Toohey (Boing Boing): [Boing Boing] In 1932, John W. Campbell imagined machines ou...
  • Anna Washenko (Engadget): [Engadget] Another parent has filed a wrongful death suit ag...
  • Associated Press (STAT News): [STAT News] FDA OKs first new sunscreen ingredient in more t...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Xat’sull First Nation bringing Calli...

Additional sources provided:

  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. man fined $6,000 for illegally ...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] After seeing Parkinson's take his father,...
  • Mohamed Hussein (Al Jazeera English): [Al Jazeera English] Iran’s lakes are vanishing: Satellite i...
  • Madison Dapcevich (Outside Online): [Outside Online] Officials Have Officially Banned Tents at a...
  • https://www.techradar.com/author/craig-hale (TechRadar): [TechRadar] Nashville Zoo leads fight against mega AI data c...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Web Search: Brave Search API
Weather Data: Open-Meteo
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [BC Gov News] Opening new homes for Indigenous families in Prince Rupert and [Engadget] German court holds Google liable for false AI Overview answers, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Should Indigenous knowledge systems be integrated into existing technology and data infrastructure, or should the conceptual framework itself be fundamentally reimagined starting from Indigenous land-based governance? Topics include: Integration vs. foundational reimagining of Indigenous knowledge systems in technology, Data sovereignty, OCAP principles, and the limits of encoding relational knowledge, Institutional independence and funding relationships as constraints on accountability, Community-led infrastructure ownership versus service delivery models, Specific case studies: Animikii, FirstVoices, Tŝilhqot'in, Lhtako Dené Nation, and BCFNJC Police Accountability Unit.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Denisiqi Services Society

Sources discussed:

  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Opening new homes for Indigenous families in P...
  • Lawrence Bonk (Engadget): [Engadget] German court holds Google liable for false AI Ove...
  • Newsletter: n***@animikii.com: Robots behaving badly VS what the land knows

Additional sources provided:

  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Clearwater mayor speaks at national ...
  • Jason Weisberger (Boing Boing): [Boing Boing] Nancy Pelosi has no time for Mike Lindell's ha...
  • Gaby Del Valle (The Verge): [The Verge] Congress just gave DHS another $70 billion
  • rebeccadyok (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Rotary Peace Pole to be installed in...
  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Sharing local farm harvests through coupon pro...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/ruth-jones (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] Anxiety and stress were keeping me awake — unti...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] I gave up my career at a Fortune 500 comp...
  • Chloe Gerrard (Newsletter: n***@animikii.com): Genome Canada, Genome Prairie Launch $50M National Resources...
  • Jordy Cunningham (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Indigenous policing accountability p...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Web Search: Brave Search API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)

Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Gear, Gadgets And Practical Tech
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Gear, Gadgets & Practical Tech.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Al Jazeera English] South Korean police break up 35-hour polling station protest and [Al Jazeera English] Ebola border shutdown leaves goods rotting between Uganda and DRC, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural communities like the Cariboo access reliable information about which gear and gadgets actually perform durably in remote, cold conditions without manufacturer support or institutional resources? Topics include: Battery chemistry performance in cold climates and manufacturer lock-in practices, The gap between institutional equipment specifications (SAR, wildfire) and what individual rural users can access, Formal vs. informal knowledge infrastructure and the discoverability problem, Structural sustainability of community databases and the handoff from student projects to permanent hosting, Municipal infrastructure as a persistence layer for community knowledge versus grant-dependent websites.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cedar Crest Society for Community Living

Sources discussed:

  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] South Korean police break up 35-hour po...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Ebola border shutdown leaves goods rott...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Bolivia approves military measures agai...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Sherpa found alive on Mount Everest aft...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Huge fire erupts at Honda warehouse in ...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocks southern...
  • Cheyenne MacDonald (Engadget): [Engadget] Astronomers measure the mass of a dormant black h...

Additional sources provided:

  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Pope Leo urges youth to be ‘sparks of a...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Israeli forces kill nine in Tyre after ...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Tunisians protest for press freedom and...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Gaza fishermen stay afloat with dinghie...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] London activists try to erect statue of...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Pope Leo welcomed in Barcelona with tra...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Smoke rises in illegal Israeli settleme...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Protesters torch cars, buildings in Bel...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Video shows Israeli soldier and settler...

Credits

Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Gear, Gadgets & Practical Tech.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Al Jazeera English] South Korean police break up 35-hour polling station protest and [Al Jazeera English] Ebola border shutdown leaves goods rotting between Uganda and DRC, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural communities like the Cariboo access reliable information about which gear and gadgets actually perform durably in remote, cold conditions without manufacturer support or institutional resources? Topics include: Battery chemistry performance in cold climates and manufacturer lock-in practices, The gap between institutional equipment specifications (SAR, wildfire) and what individual rural users can access, Formal vs. informal knowledge infrastructure and the discoverability problem, Structural sustainability of community databases and the handoff from student projects to permanent hosting, Municipal infrastructure as a persistence layer for community knowledge versus grant-dependent websites.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).
